steps.title: Use the diameter midpoint
steps.content: The center is the midpoint of the diameter endpoints $(a,10)$ and $(a,d)$, so their $y$-coordinates average to $16$.

steps.title: Solve for the endpoint
steps.content: $$\frac{10+d}{2}=16$$$$10+d=32$$$$d=22$$This gives the other endpoint's $y$-coordinate. The answer is D.
